title: platanthera orbiculata var. lehorsii
intro: Platanthera orbiculata var. lehorsii, also known as Lehmann's purple fringed orchid, is a rare and endangered species of orchid that is native to the western United States. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, coughs, and respiratory issues due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Platanthera orbiculata var. lehorsii include its potential to alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and other respiratory conditions, as well as its ability to support immune function and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be made by drying and processing the plant's roots and flowers.
